title: Multi-omic single-cell velocity models epigenome-transcriptome interactions and improves cell fate prediction
method_summary: Joint ODE model over chromatin accessibility, unspliced RNA, and spliced RNA with per-gene switch times and Model 1 / Model 2 classification.
assay_summary: Paired ATAC+RNA single-cell multiome with peak-to-gene aggregation and WNN smoothing.
main_results: Improves cell-fate flow consistency over RNA-only velocity.
Defines priming and decoupling intervals and separates genes into Model 1 and Model 2 regimes.
limitations: Temporal outputs remain in arbitrary latent time rather than wall-clock time.
No uncertainty interval for switch-time estimates.
Binary gene regime framing may hide continuous lag structure.

project_relevance.activation_lag: Priming interval is a direct precursor concept.
project_relevance.shutdown_lag: Decoupling interval partially maps to shutdown lag.
project_relevance.drug_timing_prediction: Not validated for perturbation response timing.

title: Inferring differential dynamics from multi-lineage, multi-omic, and multi-sample single-cell data with MultiVeloVAE
method_summary: Variational extension of MultiVelo with continuous cell-specific chromatin and transcription rate parameters and posterior-based inference.
assay_summary: Multi-sample single-cell ATAC+RNA with integrated preprocessing, cell-specific dynamics, and posterior-based differential testing.
main_results: Moves from discrete gene-level coupling states to continuous cell-specific coupling and decoupling factors.
Supports lineage-aware and sample-aware inference with posterior-based testing.
limitations: Dynamic outputs remain latent/model-derived rather than real-time calibrated.
Decoupling factor is lag-adjacent but not an explicit activation/shutdown lag target.

project_relevance.activation_lag: Useful as a continuous comparator, but not identical to explicit activation lag.
project_relevance.shutdown_lag: Cell-specific decoupling factor may capture repression-side asynchrony.
project_relevance.drug_timing_prediction: Suggestive for downstream modeling, but not directly validated.

title: Multi-omic relay velocity modeling uncovers dynamic chromatin-transcription regulation across cell states
method_summary: Relay velocity model that estimates cell-specific kinetics and interpretable chromatin-to-RNA lag without a single fixed global latent-time axis.
assay_summary: Single-cell multiome trajectory modeling with explicit chromatin context and signed lag interpretation.
main_results: Positive and negative chromatin-to-spliced RNA lag is interpreted directly at the gene level.
Captures developmental trajectories and asynchronous repression beyond fixed latent-time approaches.
limitations: Does not model long-range enhancer-promoter interaction, transcriptional memory, or motif-level regulation.
Inference is gene-wise and may miss pathway-level coordination.
Step 3 style perturbation validation is still absent.

project_relevance.activation_lag: Most direct existing analog to explicit activation lag estimation.
project_relevance.shutdown_lag: Negative lag and asynchronous repression are directly discussed.
project_relevance.drug_timing_prediction: Provides lag targets, but not a completed validation framework.
